steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase "To that end" signals continuation of the original goal — robots are designed to resemble humans so people will find it easier to interact with them. The blank must describe an action that supports this goal.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank must mean "strengthen or reinforce" — bipedal locomotion should bolster people's comfort with the robot. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — mitigate: reduce or lessen — this would mean features reduce feelings of comfort, the opposite of the design goal. ✗
  • Choice B — constrict: tighten or restrict — restricting feelings of comfort contradicts the goal of making interaction easier. ✗
  • Choice C — repudiate: reject or deny — repudiating comfort means dismissing it entirely, which makes no sense in this design context. ✗
  • Choice D — buttress: strengthen or support — bipedal locomotion supports and reinforces people's feelings of comfort, helping them relate to the robot. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
